subject: Ecology
title: Community Gardens
description: A quick, practical guide to Community Gardens.

hook.text: A community garden on a vacant lot can boost biodiversity more than a manicured city lawn.

buildup.text: Diverse plantings attract pollinators, birds, and beneficial insects that lawns repel.

discovery.text: Gardens also build soil health, absorb rainwater, and reduce local temperatures.

twist.text: Studies show neighborhoods with community gardens have lower stress levels and stronger ties.

climax.text: Havana, Cuba produces over half its fresh vegetables in urban gardens—proof of scale.

punchline.text: A garden feeds people and ecosystems at once.

quiz.question: How do community gardens benefit urban ecology?
options: They boost pollinator and soil biodiversity
